title: Amitriptyline: MedlinePlus Drug Information

What special precautions should I follow? 
Before taking amitriptyline, tell your doctor and pharmacist if you are allergic to amitriptyline or any other medications. Some medications should not be taken with amitriptyline. Other medications may cause dosing changes or extra monitoring when taken with amitriptyline. Make sure you have discussed any medications you are currently taking or plan to take before starting amitriptyline with your doctor and pharmacist. Before starting, stopping, or changing any medications while taking amitriptyline, please get the advice of your doctor or pharmacist.. Tell your doctor or pharmacist if you are taking the following medications or have stopped taking them within the past two weeks: monoamine oxidase (MAO) inhibitors such as isocarboxazid (Marplan), phenelzine (Nardil), selegiline (Eldepryl, Emsam, Zelapar), and tranylcypromine (Parnate). The following nonprescription products may interact with amitriptyline: allergy medications (diphenhydramine, loratadine, fexofenadine, chlorpheniramine or cetirizine); cimetidine (Tagamet). Be sure to let your doctor and pharmacist know that you are taking these medications before you start taking amitriptyline. Do not start any of these medications while taking amitriptyline without discussing with your healthcare provider.. tell yo
